title: Efficiency
short: The fraction of input energy a machine turns into useful output, a number between 0 and 1.
full:
Efficiency is useful output energy divided by input energy — a dimensionless fraction often written as a percentage. No real engine reaches 1 (100%): some energy is always lost, usually as waste heat.

For a heat engine working between a hot reservoir at T_h and a cold one at T_c, the second law caps the efficiency at the Carnot value η = 1 − T_c/T_h, with the temperatures in kelvin. Raising T_h or lowering T_c raises the ceiling.

Being a ratio, efficiency has no units.
